revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image: a 64x64 pixel-art game terrain autotile sheet, 4 columns x 4 rows, 16 frames total, each frame exactly 16x16 pixels. Use a crisp SNES-era 16-bit pixel-art look with hard square pixels, no anti-alias blur, consistent palette, top-left light, and dark one-pixel outlines where useful. No visible grid lines, borders, tile IDs, labels, UI, text, watermark, characters, creatures, weapons, modern objects, or third-party franchise likenesses.

Subject: seamless top-down Japanese fantasy RPG autotile sheet for a sacred shrine plateau retaining wall, maintained and intentional, clearly distinct from rough rural terrace walls. Cool gray dressed ashlar stone, orderly stacked geometric blocks, mossy joints and lichen, warm golden-hour light. Palette anchors: cool grey stone #8a857a, moss green #6a8a52, deep shadow #2a2820, bright cap highlight #d8d0bc, warm grass lip #7ab85a. Use solid opaque #FF00FF magenta only in empty space around tile shapes/corners; filled wall-face tiles may fill their frame.

Important scale adaptation: because each frame is 16x16, compress the requested wall structure proportionally: top 3-4 pixels = bright lit stone cap edge with warm grass lip above and strong highlight on upper rim; middle 9-10 pixels = clean vertical stone face with neat horizontal mortar lines and subtle vertical joints; bottom 2-3 pixels = deep cool shadow pooling at ground contact. Every frame must stay inside its own 16x16 cell and keep the same scale.

Layout semantics:
Row 1 frames 0-3: center fill variations, full wall face head-on, dressed ashlar-style stone, mossy joints, subtle lichen, same scale, varied block patterns.
Row 2 frames 4-7: edge tiles. Frame 4 N = flat top cap seen from above. Frame 5 S = key front-facing tile with full bright top rim, full vertical stone face, deep bottom shadow. Frame 6 W = left curved outer corner. Frame 7 E = right curved outer corner.
Row 3 frames 8-11: outer corner tiles NW, NE, SW, SE with curved convex corners, not hard 90-degree angles; south-facing corners should show bright cap, vertical face, and shadow with a three-point corner feel.
Row 4 frames 12-15: accent variations: alternate stone patterns, extra moss, lichen clusters, accent stones, small maintained shrine-wall details, still cohesive and usable as terrain tiles.

Tilemap quality: do not make repeated square stamps; vary texture, edge breakup, moss, stone-lighting, and chipped details while keeping the set cohesive and seamless. Transition shapes should blend naturally rather than forming hard rectangular bands. The final sheet should read as hand-painted Ghibli-influenced shrine geography in original SNES-style pixel art, bright and cheerful, no isometric perspective, no vanishing point, no dark gloomy palette.
